summary: Tar operations are failing on Go module cache files in go/pkg/mod/, with 32,326 errors across multiple dependency modules. This is blocking the CI job during dependency processing.
root_cause: Go module cache files in go/pkg/mod/ are failing tar operations. The pattern of failures across thousands of files (k8s.io/controller-manager, k8s.io/kubernetes, golang.org/x/*, google.golang.org/*, etc.) suggests corrupted, inaccessible, or incomplete cached artifacts.

evidence.reason: Explicit tar command failures on Go module paths in cache directory
High volume of errors concentrated in a short timespan indicates systematic failure during setup phase, not runtime test failures
Error pattern repeats across many distinct Go modules, pointing to a cache or extraction layer issue rather than individual module problems

suggested_fix: Clear the Go module cache and force re-download of dependencies: `go clean -modcache && go mod download`. If using Docker layer caching, invalidate or rebuild the dependency layer.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
